citation: Dionne, K., & The Arcane Library. (2024). “Shadowdark RPG” Series by Kelsey Dionne and The Arcane Library.
summary:
# Summary

The provided text is a promotional and informational document associated with the GameRT (Games-Based Library Services) initiative, specifically detailing a series of live-streamed Tabletop Role-Playing Game (TTRPG) sessions featuring "Shadowdark RPG" by Kelsey Dionne and The Arcane Library. The document does not present empirical research data but rather serves as a curated resource hub for library patrons and gaming enthusiasts interested in the Old School Renaissance (OSR) genre of role-playing games.

**Purpose and Context**
The primary objective of the text is to introduce "Shadowdark RPG" to an audience that may be familiar with Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition (5E) or traditional old-school gaming. The description characterizes the game as a system that allows players to "delve into mysterious ruins, lost cities, and monster-infested depths," emphasizing themes of danger and exploration ("don't let your last torch burn out"). It positions the game as a hybrid experience: an "intuitive and complete TTRPG" for 5E players seeking a bridge to OSR mechanics, while simultaneously offering "major quality-of-life upgrades" that modernize the nostalgic experience for veteran old-school gamers.

**Methodology and Data Sources**
The core "data" presented consists of three specific live-streamed sessions hosted on Twitch by GameRT. These sessions are documented with precise dates and video-on-demand (VOD) links:
1.  Session #1: Streamed on Saturday, January 13, 2024.
2.  Session #2: Streamed on Saturday, January 27, 2024.
3.  Session #3 and Q&A: Streamed on Saturday, February 10, 2024.

The text also identifies the specific adventure module used during these sessions: "Puzzle Dungeon: The Seers Sanctum" by directsun, noted as a system-agnostic OSR adventure. This indicates a methodological choice to use accessible, genre-specific content to demonstrate the game's mechanics in practice.

**Resources and Comparative Analysis**
A significant portion of the text is dedicated to providing contextual resources for viewers. It categorizes recommendations into "Play Alikes," "Read Alikes," and "View Alikes." 
*   **Play Alikes:** Lists comparable TTRPGs such as *Knave 2nd Ed*, *Mausritter*, *Rouge Second Edition*, *Ten Candles*, *Troika! Numinous Edition*, *Trophy*, *Vast in the Dark*, and *Dungeon Solitaire*. It also draws parallels to video games like *Plague Tale*, *Elden Ring*, and *Darkest Dungeon*, suggesting thematic or mechanical similarities that might appeal to non-TTRPG audiences.
*   **Read/View Alikes:** Recommends literary and visual media including *Kings of the Wyld*, *White Plume Mountain*, *Berserk*, *The Faraway Paladin*, *Frieren: Beyond Journey's End*, *Made in Abyss*, and *Record of Lodoss War*. This curation aims to situate "Shadowdark RPG" within a broader fantasy media ecosystem.

**Educational and Safety Frameworks**
The document places strong emphasis on best practices for running TTRPG sessions, particularly regarding inclusivity and safety. It provides links to resources for conducting "Session Zero," character creation, and Game Master (GM) techniques. Crucially, it lists specific safety tools such as "Script Change" by Beau Jágr Sheldon, "Lines and Veils," "The Door is Open" by Eirik Fatland, and the "TTRPG Safety Toolkit." This inclusion underscores a commitment to creating safe, consensual gaming environments, which is a critical component of library-based gaming services.

**Community Guidelines**
Finally, the text outlines the GameRT Twitch Policy, emphasizing respect, zero tolerance for hate speech, and procedures for handling harassment. It provides contact information for moderators and directs users to broader GameRT resources, including blogs, social media pages, and a Zotero collection, reinforcing the institutional support behind these gaming events.

In summary, this document functions as a comprehensive guide for engaging with "Shadowdark RPG" through the lens of library services. It combines promotional material with practical educational resources, aiming to facilitate both participation in and understanding of OSR-style tabletop gaming within a safe and structured community environment.
sourceText:
“Shadowdark RPG” Series by Kelsey Dionne and The Arcane Library
“In Shadowdark RPG, you and your group of crawlers use magic, steel, and wits to delve into mysterious ruins, lost cities, and monster-infested depths. Wondrous treasures and long-forgotten secrets await you! But don't let your last torch burn out, or you could be swallowed by the Shadowdark…. 5E players will find what they've been waiting for: an intuitive and complete TTRPG that serves as a seamless bridge into the heart of the Old School Renaissance. Old-school gamers will find a system that is familiar and nostalgic, but with major quality-of-life
upgrades that modernize the old-school experience. Your legend awaits, crawler. Lift your blade, light your torch, and plunge into the unknown depths of the Shadowdark!”
Watch the VODs of these sessions here: Session #1, originally streamed Saturday, January 13, 2024: https://www.twitch.tv/videos/2031578075 Session #2, originally streamed Saturday, January 27, 2024: https://www.twitch.tv/videos/2045484415 Session #3 and Q&A, originally streamed Saturday, February 10, 2024: https://www.twitch.tv/videos/2062893635 https://www.twitch.tv/videos/2062897187

RESOURCES

The Game Shadowdark RPG by Kelsey Dionne and The Arcane Library
● Shadowdark RPG’s Kickstarter campaign page ● Published adventure used (system agnostic OSR adventure): “Puzzle Dungeon: The
Seers Sanctum” by directsun

Actual Plays ● Live Play Example: Shadowdark RPG ● ShadowDark RPG - One Shot Part 1 &

ShadowDark RPG - One Shot Part 2

Articles ● Interview with Kelsey Dionne ● How To Get 5E D&D Players to Try A New RPG System ● What is OSR? A newcomer’s guide to the Old School Renaissance RPGs

Play Alikes ● TTRPGs ○ Knave 2nd Ed by Ben Milton ○ Mausritter by Losing Games ○ Rouge Second Edition by Kazumi Chin ○ Ten Candles by Cavalry Games ○ Troika! Numinous Edition by Melsonian Arts Council ○ Trophy by The Gauntlet ○ Vast in the Dark by Feral Indie Studio ○ Dungeon Solitaire by Matthew Lowes ● Video games ○ Plague Tale by Focus Entertainment ○ Elden Ring by Bandai Namco ○ Darkest Dungeon by Red Hook Studios

Read Alikes ● Books ○ Kings of the Wyld by Nicholas Eames ○ White Plume Mountain by Paul Kidd ● Graphic Novels, Comics, & Manga ○ Berserk by Kentaro Miura ○ The Faraway Paladin by Mutsumi Okubashi

View Alikes ● The Faraway Paladin ● Frieren: Beyond Journey's End ● Made in Abyss ● Record of Lodoss War
Helpful Links ● Session zero ○ GameRT Session Zero Webinar & Resource Slides ○ How to run a session zero ○ New Player’s Guide: How to Make the Most of Session Zero - D&D Beyond ○ Running Session Zeros: SlyFlourish.com ○ Session Zero: A Foundation for Greatness - D&D Beyond ○ The Ultimate D&D Session 0 Checklist ● Character creation ○ Ultimate Character Guides by James Damato ○ Roleplaying Tips by John Four ● GMing for every kind of player ○ Ultimate Game Master Guides by James Damato ○ Return of the Lazy DM by Sly Flourish ○ Adventuring Academy: Interviews!
Safety Tools ● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● Guest Blog: An Introduction to Lines and Veils ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive ● Consent in Gaming by Sean K. Reynolds and Shanna Germain
GameRT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason

4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please ask a GameRT Twitch mod for assistance 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Zotero: https://bit.ly/GameRTZotero Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
